review_method: git worktree checkout of e30c7bb at /tmp/.../scratchpad/review-cluster-e (node_modules symlinked from main repo checkout ā€” package.json/pnpm-lock.yaml unchanged in this commit, diff confirmed 0 lines), full-file reads of every changed source file, line-by-line diff inspection, grep sweeps for prohibited patterns, and a fresh full vitest/tsc/next-build run inside the worktree.

uptime_removed.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE-2026-07-18T07:04Z] `grep -rn "99\.8" apps/web/src` at e30c7bb returns exactly ONE hit: apps/web/src/components/agents/Orchestration.tsx:109, which is inside the explanatory code comment documenting the removal ("... the fabricated \"uptime 99.8%\" literal has been removed ..."), not a live literal. The rendered header string at Orchestration.tsx:111 is `{online} agents online Ā· {queued} task{...} in queue` ā€” no percentage, no uptime token. `online` (Orchestration.tsx:63) = `agents.filter(a => a.status !== "offline").length` and `queued` (Orchestration.tsx:64) = `runs.filter(r => r.status === "running" || r.status === "queued").length` ā€” both derived from the live `agents`/`runs` props passed down from the Agents page's real API fetches, not fabricated. Scanned the rest of Orchestration.tsx for other hardcoded-metric literals: successRate/avgSecs/tasks-run counts are all computed from the `runs` prop (pre-existing, untouched by this diff, already carry MV-agent-monitor-002/003 honesty disclosures); 'Pause All'/'Manual Override' buttons remain honestly disabled (pre-existing, untouched). No new fabricated metric introduced.

feed_approve_no_raw_leak.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE-2026-07-18T07:04Z] dashboard/page.tsx:167 `pendingApprovalIds = new Set(pending.map(a => a.id))` is built from `approvals.data` which is loaded via `fetchApprovals("pending")` ā€” a live, independent GET /approvals?status=pending fetch (page.tsx:140) ā€” NOT the AgentRun snapshot. page.tsx:292-296 gates `isCoverLetterPending` on BOTH the cached `out.approval_status === "pending"` AND `pendingApprovalIds.has(out.approval_id)`, so a resolved-elsewhere approval's inline button disappears even though the AgentRun snapshot still says 'pending'. On error (page.tsx:180), `describeApprovalError` (defined page.tsx:88-93) replaces the OLD code's `e instanceof Error ? e.message : "request failed"` (which directly leaked `ApiError.message`, confirmed at lib/api/client.ts:121-122 to be literally `${method} ${path} failed (${status}): ${detail}` ā€” i.e. raw HTTP method/endpoint/status/JSON body) with a closed set of generic, honest strings (409 -> 'already handled', 404 -> 'no longer exists', anything else -> generic 'try again'); no branch of describeApprovalError ever surfaces e.message. page.tsx:187 additionally drops the id from `approvals.data` on 409/404 so the shared pending set (also feeding the separate 'Needs Approval' widget at page.tsx:571-630) stays consistent everywhere, not just in the feed. Confirmed via fresh vitest run: page.test.tsx's two error-path tests construct a real ApiError with the exact raw-leak-shaped message string ('POST /approvals/appr-1/approve failed (409): {"detail":...}') and assert the rendered toast text does NOT match /POST|GET|\/approvals\/|409|500|\{.*detail/i, and DOES match /already/i or /couldn.?t approve/i respectively ā€” a non-tautological, adversarial assertion. All 4 tests in this file passed fresh in this run.

quota_real.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE-2026-07-18T07:04Z] sidebar.tsx:7 imports `fetchSubscription`/`SubscriptionState` from `../lib/api/billing`; lib/api/billing.ts defines `SubscriptionStateSchema` (zod) and `fetchSubscription()` which calls `apiRequest<unknown>("/billing/subscription", options)` and parses the response through the schema ā€” a real, pre-existing, already-in-use backend endpoint (also consumed by the Settings page per the fix's own comment), not a new or fabricated source. sidebar.tsx's new effect (lines ~52-64) sets `subscription` to `undefined` (loading) / real value / `null` (on any fetch rejection, i.e. honest failure, never a fabricated number) via `.catch(() => setSubscription(null))`. The render block (sidebar.tsx ~114-131) shows 'Checking planā€¦' while loading, 'Plan unavailable' on `null`, otherwise the real `subscription.plan?.name` and, only if `subscription.quota` exists, the real `runsUsed/runsAllowed` pair; if quota is absent it shows honest 'No usage quota on record' text rather than inventing figures. No `Math.random()` or literal numeric fallback anywhere in the new code (grep of all added diff lines for Math.random found only comment mentions confirming its absence). Fresh vitest run: sidebar.test.tsx (3 tests, all pass) covers (a) real plan name + real 12/50 quota rendering, (b) the no-quota-on-record honest-fallback path with a plan present but quota null, and (c) the fetch-rejection -> 'Plan unavailable' path ā€” none of these are tautological; each supplies a distinct mocked SubscriptionState/rejection and asserts the corresponding distinct honest UI text.

import_change_behaviorless.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE-2026-07-18T07:04Z] sidebar.tsx:6,8 changed `@/lib/api/agents` -> `../lib/api/agents` and `@/lib/navigation` -> `../lib/navigation`. apps/web/tsconfig.json maps `@/*` -> `./src/*`; from `apps/web/src/components/sidebar.tsx`, `@/lib/api/agents` and `../lib/api/agents` both resolve to the identical file `apps/web/src/lib/api/agents.ts` (one path segment up from `components/` into `src/`, same as `./src/lib/api/agents` under the alias) ā€” confirmed by directory structure inspection, not merely assumed. This is not a cosmetic-only change: apps/web/vitest.config.ts has no `resolve.alias` entry and the repo has no `vite-tsconfig-paths` (or equivalent) plugin dependency, so vitest cannot resolve the `@/*` tsconfig path mapping at all ā€” a component using `@/...` imports cannot be unit-tested in this project's current vitest setup. topbar.tsx and user-menu.tsx (both pre-existing, already tested) already used relative imports for exactly this reason, confirmed by reading their pre-fix source. The import switch was therefore a necessary precondition for the new sidebar.test.tsx to run at all, follows the codebase's established convention, and changes zero logic ā€” verified by diffing sidebar.tsx e30c7bb^ vs e30c7bb: the only non-import, non-JSX-block changes are the fetchSubscription effect/state addition and the new plan/quota JSX block (both in-scope for MV-dashboard-006); the pre-existing agent-pulse logic (fetchAgents effect, `running`/`agentsActive` derivation, the 'Agents Active/Idle' card, the 'Manage Agents' link) is reproduced verbatim, only re-indented/reordered under the new wrapping `flex flex-col gap-3` div alongside the new quota card.

tests_meaningful.evidence: [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE-2026-07-18T07:04Z] Spot-checked all 5 changed/added test files by full read (not just diff hunks): dashboard/__tests__/page.test.tsx (4 tests) constructs REAL ApiError instances with realistic raw-leak-shaped messages and asserts both a negative match (no raw HTTP tokens) and a positive match (honest copy) plus the stale-approval-hidden / still-pending-shown behavioral pair ā€” not tautological. sidebar.test.tsx (3 tests) exercises three distinct SubscriptionState shapes (populated, quota-absent-with-plan, fetch-rejected) each asserting a DIFFERENT honest-UI outcome. topbar.test.tsx (4 tests, jsdom class-presence, limitation self-disclosed in file header with cited precedent) and user-menu.test.tsx (2 new tests) verify the exact Tailwind utility tokens (`truncate`, `min-h-16` present + `h-16` absent, `max-lg:hidden`) that were independently confirmed (see mobile_header_fixed) to produce the claimed CSS behavior ā€” not asserting an arbitrary class name disconnected from behavior. orchestration.test.tsx (2 new tests) asserts both the NEGATIVE (no 'uptime' text, no '99.8%' anywhere in the section) and the POSITIVE (real online/queued counts still present) ā€” this pairing rules out a fixer simply deleting the whole header line to make a naive 'not.toMatch' test pass. No test file uses a broad/no-op assertion (e.g. `expect(true).toBe(true)`, snapshot-only, or asserting on values the component trivially always returns).

prohibited_patterns_notes: grep of all ADDED lines in the diff (git diff e30c7bb^ e30c7bb) for eslint-disable, @ts-ignore/@ts-expect-error, `as any`/`: any`, Math.random(), TODO/FIXME, --no-verify, placeholder/mock/fake found: (1) 3x `// eslint-disable-next-line import/first` in the NEW test files (page.test.tsx:112, sidebar/topbar/user-menu test files similarly) ā€” narrowly scoped to the single import-after-vi.mock line required by vitest's hoisting pattern; confirmed this is a PRE-EXISTING codebase convention with 11 other instances in unrelated test files on main (dashboard/jobs, applications, networking, settings, login, signup, pricing page.test.tsx files), not a new suppression introduced to hide a real defect in this diff. (2) 2x 'Math.random()' and 1x '99.8%'/'fake number' ā€” all three are inside explanatory code comments describing what was REMOVED/avoided, not live code. No new `any` casts, no `@ts-ignore`/`@ts-expect-error`, no `--no-verify`, no new placeholder/mock content reachable by a user, no fabricated scores.
reasons: Orchestration.tsx:106-111 ā€” fabricated 'uptime 99.8%' literal fully removed; online/queued counts (Orchestration.tsx:63-64) are real live-prop derivations; repo-wide grep confirms no residual live '99.8' literal.

dashboard/page.tsx:88-93,167,180,187,292-296 ā€” inline Approve button now gated on the live GET /approvals?status=pending set (pendingApprovalIds), not the stale AgentRun snapshot alone; on 409/404 the stale id is dropped from shared state so no widget keeps offering a dead action.

dashboard/page.tsx:88-93 vs lib/api/client.ts:121-122 ā€” describeApprovalError never surfaces ApiError.message (which is proven to contain raw method+path+status+JSON detail); replaces the prior code's direct `e.message` leak with a closed set of honest generic strings; confirmed by 2 tests that assert the toast text does not match raw-HTTP regex patterns against a realistic raw ApiError.

topbar.tsx:228,243-244 and user-menu.tsx:77 ā€” header no longer clips at narrow viewports (min-h-16 + truncate + min-w-0); identity-chip text hidden only below the lg (1024px) breakpoint while the account-menu button/avatar/aria-label remain reachable at all widths; default Tailwind breakpoints (no custom screens config) confirm zero behavioral change at desktop widths (>=1024px), matching the pre-fix appearance there.

sidebar.tsx:7,52-64,108-131 ā€” plan/quota indicator sourced from the real, pre-existing, zod-schema-validated GET /billing/subscription (lib/api/billing.ts fetchSubscription); honest 'Checking planā€¦'/'Plan unavailable'/'No usage quota on record' fallbacks on loading/error/missing-quota; no fabricated numbers or Math.random() found in the new code.

sidebar.tsx:6,8 ā€” @/ -> relative import switch resolves to byte-identical target files under tsconfig's @/*->./src/* mapping; independently justified by vitest.config.ts lacking any @/ alias resolution (no tsconfig-paths plugin), matching the relative-import convention topbar.tsx/user-menu.tsx already used before this fix; the only non-import changes in sidebar.tsx are the in-scope MV-dashboard-006 quota feature, with the pre-existing agent-pulse card logic reproduced verbatim.

Fresh full-suite vitest run inside an isolated worktree at e30c7bb: 390/390 tests green across 54 files, including all 5 changed/added test files by name; tsc --noEmit clean; next build clean (compiles, lints, generates all 29 routes).

No prohibited patterns in any added line: no @ts-ignore/@ts-expect-error, no new any-casts, no Math.random() metrics, no --no-verify, no placeholder/mock content reachable by users, no fabricated scores; the 3 new eslint-disable-next-line import/first comments match an established, narrowly-scoped, pre-existing codebase convention (11 prior instances) for the vi.hoisted mock-then-import test pattern, not a novel suppression of a real issue.

Tests are non-tautological: each spot-checked assertion supplies a distinct, realistic input (a real ApiError with raw-leak-shaped text, three distinct SubscriptionState shapes, negative+positive text-match pairs) and asserts a distinct, specific, correct outcome rather than a value the code trivially always returns.
